## Compact Rules by Agent

Delegators copy matching blocks into sub-agent prompts under Project Standards (auto-resolved).

### BA - Analysis

#### backlog-management-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### business

- No compact rules extracted

#### customer-success-manager

- Use NPS and CSAT consistently (same questions, same frequency)

#### customer-support-lead

- Never blame the user ("you should have..." → "let me help you...")
- Use their name and reference their specific issue

#### data-analyst

- Always validate joins (one-to-many creates duplicates)
- Check for NULL handling in aggregations (SUM ignores NULLs, COUNT doesn't)
- Never report on incomplete data without a caveat

#### product-manager

- Never surprise stakeholders — communicate delays early

#### project-manager

- Verify all deliverables meet acceptance criteria
- Never hide delays — communicate within 4 hours of identifying

### BUS-TELE - Telemetry

#### business-telemetry-skill

- Extract:: Read local session artifacts (`docs/sessions/`, `docs/judgment/`).
- Identify:: Retrieve `User_ID` from Git configuration.
- Analyze:: Determine the `Efficiency_Score` based on token usage vs. successful judgment.
- Append:: Add a new row to `docs/management/telemetry-master.csv`.
- Report:: If significant anomalies are found (e.g., high tokens, repeated failures), add a note

#### backend-engineer

- NEVER trust user input (validate and sanitize)
- Use parameterized queries (prevent SQL injection)
- Use proper HTTP methods (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE)
- Use indexes wisely (not everywhere)

#### daily-workflow

- Check public repo sync: `cd gentle-vanguard-public && git pull`
- Check stack health: `gv verify`
- Review audits: Check `docs/audits/` for recent reports
- Sync public repo: Ensure installer and docs are current
- Context efficiency: Monitor prompt chars and adoption rate

- No compact rules extracted

#### script-governance-skill

- MUST use verb-noun naming (e.g., detect-ide-session.ps1)
- MUST keep utility scripts in scripts/utilities/
- MUST keep scripts idempotent — safe to run multiple times
- MUST NOT mutate state in detection scripts
- MUST log all state mutations in activation scripts
- MUST resolve repository root dynamically with git rev-parse --show-toplevel in hooks
- MUST expose clear status output (what failed, why, exact command to fix)
- MUST validate file paths before execution
- MUST keep logs deterministic for auditability

#### script-runtime-engineering-skill

- Use `${var}` delimiters when variable boundaries can be ambiguous.
- Avoid modern-only constructs.
- Keep messages and separators ASCII when possible.

### FINANCE - Finance

#### finance-financial-analyst

- Check for reasonableness (sanity checks)
- Never hardcode numbers that should be formulas
- Use consistent time periods (monthly vs quarterly) across all statements
- Never bury bad scenarios — decision-makers need realism

### GOV - Governance

#### _semantic-skill-matcher

- Use `context7_query-docs` for additional context
- Never return a skill with score <0.5 (too weak)
- Always explain WHY a skill matched (keywords, concepts)
- User override > semantic score (user knows best)
- Use Context7 as a BOOST, not replacement (semantic is primary)

#### adaptive-mode-orchestrator

- Checkpoints automticos despus de cada fase

#### adaptive-orchestrator

- MUST execute all systems via `scripts/adaptive/*.ps1` scripts
- MUST NOT block commits (non-blocking at session boundaries)
- MUST use AES-256 encryption for backups (key derived from stack identity)
- MUST only log metadata (paths, timestamps) for doc-drift — NO code/doc content
- MUST retry max 3x before escalation to human

#### auto-delegation-router

- NEVER auto-delegate: AGENTS.md/config changes, credential/security ops, session lifecycle

#### chained-pr

- every child PR in merge/review order,
- Do not review dependent PR changes here.
- Prefer clear dependency notes over clever branch gymnastics.

#### comment-writer

- No compact rules extracted

#### commit-hygiene-skill

- Use imperative mood: "add" not "added"
- Keep subject line 72 characters

#### config-risk-analyzer

- No compact rules extracted

#### cross-workspace-sync

- User mentions "sync workspaces" or "sincronizar workspaces"
- User asks to "resolve conflict" or "resolver conflicto"

#### gentle-vanguard-audit-skill

- Never modify files during audit — only report issues

#### gentle-vanguard-manager-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### gitflow-orchestrator-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### guardian-fallback-skill

- Requires code review to close PR

#### incident-response-plan

- Preserve evidence

#### judgment-day

- MUST NOT: declare APPROVED until: Round 1 judges return CLEAN, OR Round 2+ confirms 0
- MUST NOT: push, commit, or code-modify until re-judgment completes after fixes
- MUST NOT: save summary or say "done" until every JD reaches APPROVED or ESCALATED
- MUST preserve MINORITY POSITIONS: in output — never suppress dissenting views (Gemini
- MUST flag SYCOPHANCY: when a judge changes verdict between rounds without citing new evidence
- Orchestrator NEVER reviews code itself: — only launches judges, reads results, synthesizes
- Always wait for BOTH judges to complete before synthesizing

#### multi-agent-registry

- MUST decompose tasks before routing — orchestrator does NOT execute domain work
- MUST NOT cross agent boundaries (each agent stays in its CAN/CANNOT scope per agent-definitions)
- MUST use Agent Result Schema (FF-007) for structured JSON output
- MUST escalate to orchestrator on out-of-scope requests
- MUST route via `config/subagent-mapping.json`

#### project-orchestrator-skill

- MUST execute pre-process-input.ps1 BEFORE every user response
- MUST load triggered skill before any other action
- MUST use Engram for durable memory (not session-only state)
- MUST run `gv.ps1 ide-status` -> `gv.ps1 health` -> `gv.ps1 start-session` on session start
- MUST validate spec before PR merge
- MUST generate audit document before push
- MUST NOT modify repository structure without explicit user approval
- MUST stop and notify on ambiguity before proceeding

#### security-expert-skill

- Check dependency vulnerabilities (npm audit, safety)

#### security-skill

- MUST: use parameterized queries or prepared statements — never string concatenation for SQL
- MUST: hash passwords with bcrypt or argon2
- MUST: encode output for context (HTML escape, URL encode, etc.)
- MUST: validate all external input on every entry point
- MUST: use httpOnly, secure, sameSite cookies for sessions
- MUST NOT: commit secrets, API keys, or credentials to version control
- MUST NOT: expose internal implementation details in error messages

#### self-diagnosis-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### skill-creator-skill

- Use tables for decisión trees
- Keep code examples minimal and focused
- Use web URLs in references (use local paths)

#### skill-factory-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### sync-automation

- No compact rules extracted

#### workflow-orchestrator

- NEVER execute a task whose dependencies have not completed
- Every workflow MUST have state tracking and error recovery strategy

### HR - Talent

#### hr-talent-acquisition

- Use same rubric for all candidates (no moving goalposts)
- Never ask illegal questions (age, marital status, religion)
- Use inclusive language in job descriptions
- Respect candidate's current employer (no reference checks without permission)

### LEGAL - Legal

#### legal-compliance-officer

- Never collect data "just in case" — purpose limitation is law
- Every data processing needs documented lawful basis
- Keep DPIAs (Data Protection Impact Assessments) current
- Never retaliate against users exercising rights

### MKT - Marketing

#### brand-guide-skill

- Never place logo on competing colors

#### content-strategist

- Use related keywords (LSI) not just exact match
- Keep messaging consistent across channels
- Never miss a publish date (readers expect consistency)

#### marketing-content-writer

- Use short paragraphs (2-3 sentences)
- Check grammar (Grammarly, Hemingway Editor)
- Verify all links work (internal + external)
- Never keyword stuff (reads horribly, Google penalizes)
- Use related keywords naturally (LSI keywords)
- Use approved terminology (not synonyms)
- Check sensitive topics (legal review if needed)

#### marketing-growth-hacker

- User retention and engagement improvement
- Use data to generate testable growth hypotheses
- Always have a control group for comparison
- Respect user privacy and opt-out preferences

#### seo-audit-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### seo-specialist

- Check Google Search Console (errors, queries)
- Check competitor rankings (what are they targeting?)

### OPS - DevOps

#### backup-orchestrator

- User mentions "backup strategy" or "estrategia de backup"
- User asks to "optimize backups" or "optimizar backups"

#### devops-sre

- Never blame individuals in postmortems — focus on system failures

#### docker-devops-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### incident-response-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### kubernetes-deployment

- Use resource requests and limits
- Use ConfigMaps for configuration
- Use service accounts and RBAC
- Use namespaces for isolation
- Use latest image tags in production
- Use default service accounts

#### monitoring-aggregator

- User mentions "analyze metrics" or "analizar mtricas"
- User asks to "show trends" or "mostrar tendencias"
- User requests "predict resource usage" or "predecir uso de recursos"

#### observability-skill

- Use structured JSON logs, not freeform strings
- Never log secrets, tokens, passwords, or full PII
- Use stable field names across services
- Every individual exception as a page

#### operations-manager

- No compact rules extracted

#### parallel-execution-limits

- User mentions "parallel execution", "ejecucin paralela", or "execution limits"

#### release-management-skill

- MUST use semver: MAJOR (breaking), MINOR (feature), PATCH (fix)
- MUST update CHANGELOG.md before every release
- MUST include migration notes for breaking changes
- MUST define a rollback plan before cutover
- MUST keep hotfix scope minimal — no opportunistic refactors
- MUST focus release notes on user impact, not implementation details

#### terraform-infrastructure

- Use remote state (S3, Terraform Cloud)
- Use workspaces for environments
- Use variables for flexibility
- Keep sensitive data in separate variable files
- Use hardcoded values
- Use root module for everything

### PREMORTEM - Risk

#### premortem-skill

- MUST set the premortem frame explicitly before generating failure reasons
- MUST gather sufficient context (What, For Whom, Success criteria) before executing
- MUST launch ALL deep-dive sub-agents in parallel, not sequentially
- MUST generate a visual HTML report + markdown transcript
- MUST produce a concise 3-sentence chat summary at the end
- MUST NOT soften findings — the goal is to tell the user what they don't want to hear

### QA - Testing

#### bdd-scenarios-skill

- Use tags for organization and filtering
- Keep scenarios at 3-7 steps

#### branch-pr

- Every PR MUST link an approved issue: no exceptions
- Automated checks must pass: before merge is possible
- Blank PRs without issue linkage will be blocked: by GitHub Actions
- Verify issue has `status:approved` label

#### code-review-orchestrator-skill

- MUST run Security + Quality quick scan on every pre-commit
- MUST run all 7 dimensions pre-merge
- MUST block commit on CRITICAL findings (exit code 1)
- MUST generate a report at docs/code-reviews/
- MUST classify every finding by severity
- MUST NOT modify source files during review
- MUST keep quick scan under 30 seconds

#### github-pr-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### go-testing

- No compact rules extracted

#### issue-creation

- Blank issues are disabled: MUST use a template (bug report or feature request)
- Every issue gets `status:needs-review` automatically: on creation
- A maintainer MUST add `status:approved`: before any PR can be opened
- Questions go to Discussions: of the project repository, not issues
- Check pre-flight checkboxes

#### playwright-skill

- Avoid over-engineering - minimal tests for what exists
- Prevent flaky tests - real exploration = stable tests
- Never assume how UI "should" work
- Check `tests/` for existing page objects first
- Keep under 60 lines when possible

#### pretool-format-hook-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### pytest-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### quality-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### security-pentester

- Verify findings (false positives are costly)
- Never test without written authorization (it's illegal)
- Respect scope — don't attack out-of-scope assets

#### testing-coverage-skill

- Every feature or bug fix MUST include tests
- Use setup/cleanup blocks with temp dirs

#### testing-evidence-qa

- Never "mostly pass" — it's PASS or FAIL

#### testing-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### testing-strategy-skill

- MUST follow test pyramid: 70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E
- MUST test business logic, API handlers, critical paths, and edge cases
- MUST NOT test framework internals, trivial getters, configuration, or third-party code
- MUST use Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
- MUST keep tests independent (no order dependency)
- MUST write tests in same package (Go) or alongside source (Angular)

#### verificador-datos-skill

- No compact rules extracted

### SAD - Design

#### api-design-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### architecture-governance

- Document the chosen shape in `docs/project-context.md` and `ARCHITECTURE.md`.

#### cloud-agent-connector-skill

- Load Config:: Read from `cloud-agents.local.json` or `config/cloud-agents.json`
- Auth:: Inject API keys from environment
- Dispatch:: Send with default `temperature=0.1` (configurable) and optional strict JSON mode
- Validate:: Prefer strict JSON mode (`-StrictJson`) and validate responses in caller workflows
- Log:: Record runtime telemetry to `.runtime/telemetry/cloud-agent-telemetry.csv`

#### context-engineering-skill

- MUST reference files by path instead of repeating file contents already in context
- MUST NOT include full file trees when only 2-3 files changed — use `git diff --stat`
- MUST NOT re-explain prior decisions already made — reference the session artifact
- MUST NOT load all skills and all docs at once when only 1-2 are needed

#### data-scientist

- Check for overfitting (train vs test performance)
- Never use test data during training (even for normalization)

- No compact rules extracted

#### database-relational-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### design-md

- No compact rules extracted

#### design-ui-designer

- Use fluid typography and spacing systems
- Use approved color palettes and typography only

#### design-ux-researcher

- Never assume — observe real users completing real tasks
- Every recommendation backed by specific user quotes or data

#### distributed-tracing-skill

- No compact rules extracted

#### fireworks-tech-graph

- Classify: the diagram type (see Diagram Types below)
- Extract structure: — identify layers, nodes, edges, flows, and semantic groups from user
- Plan layout: — apply the layout rules for the diagram type
- Load style reference: — always load `references/style-1-flat-icon.md` unless user specifies
- Map nodes to shapes: — use Shape Vocabulary below
- Check icon needs: — load `references/icons.md` for known products
- Write SVG: with adaptive strategy (see SVG Generation Strategy below)
- Validate: : Run `python3 -c "import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; ET.parse('file.svg')"` to
- Export PNG: : Use `cairosvg` (recommended). See **SVG → PNG Conversion** section below for full
- Report: the generated file paths
- (Optional) Visual self-review: — if your runtime can read images, load the exported PNG back

#### game-designer

- Avoid "gaslighting" players (if many report a bug, it's real)
